title: GLM-5.2 pushes open models toward long-horizon coding work
friendlyTitle: A new open AI model is built for long coding jobs
summary: Z.ai introduced GLM-5.2, an MIT-licensed flagship model focused on long-horizon tasks, coding agents, and a stable 1M-token context window. The company says architectural changes such as IndexShare reduce long-context attention cost while improving coding and agentic benchmarks.
friendlySummary: Z.ai released GLM-5.2, a model designed to handle very long tasks and large codebases. It is open under the MIT license and supports a very large context window.
whyItMatters: For builders, the practical signal is that open models are increasingly viable for extended codebase work, debugging, and research loops. Long context is most useful when it stays reliable under real engineering pressure, not just when it appears on a spec sheet.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This could help developers use open models for bigger coding and research jobs, especially when they need the model to keep track of lots of files and details.
